title: Past Lives
logline: When childhood sweethearts from Seoul reconnect online after 12 years apart, their rekindled connection forces a now-married Korean-Canadian playwright to confront the life she might have lived.
antagonistOrConflict: The pull between past and present selves, embodied by the choice between Hae Sung (her Korean past) and Arthur (her American present)

actBreaks.description: Nora cuts off contact with Hae Sung, ending their intense online reconnection and choosing to focus on her American life.

validation.ruling: Scene #46 functions as a major turning point where Nora makes the irreversible decision to cut contact with Hae Sung, fundamentally changing the story's direction and forcing both characters into a new phase of their lives separated by her choice.

actBreaks.description: Hae Sung arrives in New York, physically bringing Nora's past into her present reality for the first time in 24 years.

validation.ruling: Scene #70 marks the transition into the final act as Hae Sung arrives in New York in pouring rain, setting up the inevitable confrontation and resolution between the former childhood sweethearts after years of separation.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a triptych examining three distinct time periods: childhood in Seoul (late 90s), online reconnection as young adults (12 years later), and physical reunion in New York (another 12 years later). Each section explores the same relationship through different lenses of time, distance, and identity. The structure mirrors the Korean concept of In-Yun that Nora explains - the idea that relationships carry forward across lifetimes, with each section representing a different 'life' of the same connection. Rather than following traditional dramatic escalation, the script builds emotional intensity through accumulation and the weight of time passing.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing shifts dramatically between sections. The childhood section (scenes 2-12) moves quickly through key moments. The middle section (scenes 19-46) slows down for extended Skype conversations that build intimacy through real-time dialogue. The final section (scenes 70-103) operates in compressed time over just a few days, with longer scenes that allow for deeper emotional excavation, culminating in the extended bar conversation (scene 98) that spans 10 pages.

incitingIncident.description: Hae Sung wakes up hungover, drinks water his mom left, and reads Nora's Facebook message asking if he remembers her.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #19 shows Hae Sung drinking with friends but doesn't shatter his status quo. Scene #22 is the true inciting incident where Hae Sung wakes up hungover and reads Nora's Facebook message asking if he remembers her, which shatters his ordinary world and forces him into the main conflict of reconnecting with his childhood love.

midpoint.description: Nora and Arthur make faces at each other across a New York street, then walk away hand-in-hand as Nora reveals they're married.

validation.ruling: Position sanity: original at 37.2%. Corrected to nearest turning-point in 35-72% window.

climax.description: Nora and Hae Sung walk in heavy silence to his uber pickup location, wait for two excruciating minutes, then hug goodbye as he gets in the car.
climax.sceneHeading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ VERY LATE NIGHT ‑ ALMOST DAWN

validation.ruling: Scene #98 is just conversation at a bar, not the climactic confrontation. Scene #100 is the true climax where Nora and Hae Sung walk in heavy silence to his uber pickup, wait in excruciating tension, then share their final goodbye hug as he leaves - this is the peak emotional confrontation that resolves their relationship.

scenes.heading: INT. EAST VILLAGE BAR / SPEAKEASY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung talk intensely at a speakeasy booth while Arthur silently scrolls his phone beside them.

scenes.heading: EXT. PYOUNGCHON ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE‑90S ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Twelve-year-old Na Young cries while walking home from school with Hae Sung because she got second place in exams.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ DAD'S OFFICE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Na Young's parents pack their office while smoking, and Na Young announces she will be called Michelle in their new country.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ MOM'S OFFICE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Na Young's mom packs her office and asks Na Young who she likes at school.

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG AND HAE SUNG'S CLASSROOM ‑ ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung leans against Na Young's desk at school while she draws flowers on his arm with a pen.

scenes.heading: INT. MOM'S OFFICE ‑ NA YOUNG'S FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Na Young tells her mom she likes Hae Sung and will probably marry him while her mom examines their new passports.

scenes.heading: EXT. GWACHEON NATIONAL MUSEUM OF MODERN CONTEMPORARY ART ‑
scenes.summary: Na Young and Hae Sung visit an art museum together while their mothers watch and comment on how good they look together.

scenes.heading: EXT. GWACHEON NATIONAL MUSEUM OF MODERN CONTEMPORARY ART ‑
scenes.summary: Na Young and Hae Sung approach a sculpture of two faces gazing at each other, and Hae Sung studies Na Young's face.

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG'S MOM'S CAR ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Na Young falls asleep with her head on Hae Sung's lap in the car while their hands are tangled together.

scenes.heading: INT. NA YOUNG AND HAE SUNG'S CLASSROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: On Na Young's last day of class, she tells classmates she's immigrating and never coming back while Hae Sung sits apart, glancing over occasionally.

scenes.heading: EXT. NA YOUNG'S NEIGHBORHOOD ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung walks Na Young home for the last time, remaining cold and expressionless, then calls out to her but hesitates before saying goodbye.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final goodbye between childhood Na Young and Hae Sung before her immigration

scenes.heading: INT. PLANE TO TORONTO ‑ A FEW WEEKS LATER ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Na Young and her family practice English on the plane to Toronto, with the girls giggling as they loop "I'm fine. And you?"

scenes.heading: INT. TORONTO PEARSON AIRPORT ‑ CANADA ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: The family arrives at Toronto airport where the parents speak limited English to immigration officers while Nora sits on luggage with sleeping Michelle.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S TORONTO CLASS ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora attends her first day of school in Canada in a classroom of mostly white kids as the bell rings to begin her new life.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora's new life in Canada officially begins

scenes.heading: INT. FIELDS ‑ KOREA ‑ LUNCHTIME
scenes.summary: Hundreds of Korean men in military uniforms march through fields and eat dinner together, including an anonymous Hae Sung.

scenes.heading: INT. NYC TAXI ‑ APPROACHING NYC ‑ DUSK
scenes.summary: Twenty-four-year-old Nora rides in a taxi approaching Manhattan, staring at the gleaming city skyline in love with the view.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL ‑ CLASSROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora sits in grad school receiving feedback on her play pages while a classmate named Robert makes comments about her work.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora talks with her mom on the phone while googling childhood friends from Seoul, discovering one became a lawyer.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ 3 A.M. KST
scenes.summary: Hae Sung drinks heavily with friends at 3 AM to help one get over a breakup, then drunkenly searches for Na Young online and finds her Facebook profile.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hae Sung discovers Nora's Facebook profile and decides to reconnect

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS OF SEOUL ‑ LATER, WEE HOURS OF THE MORNING
scenes.summary: Hae Sung stumbles drunk through Seoul streets alone as dawn breaks and the city wakes up.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S FAMILY APARTMENT ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hae Sung quietly enters his family apartment, trying not to wake his parents in his drunken state.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hae Sung wakes up hungover, drinks water his mom left, and reads Nora's Facebook message asking if he remembers her.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S KITCHEN ‑ SEOUL ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hae Sung joins his parents for breakfast in their ordinary kitchen where his mom has prepared hangover soup.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL LIBRARY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ EVENING EST (MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora receives Hae Sung's punny Facebook response "Of course" and grins ear-to-ear while working in the library.

scenes.heading: EXT. UPPER WEST SIDE STREETS ‑ NEW YORK ‑ MOMENTS LATER ‑
scenes.summary: Nora walks back to her dorm with increasing speed until she finds herself running and grinning down New York streets.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM/HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ NEW YORK/SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung have their first Skype call, giggling uncontrollably like kids as they recognize each other after years apart.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct contact between Nora and Hae Sung after 12 years

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM/HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ NIGHT EST
scenes.summary: Hours later on Skype, Hae Sung says he likes talking to Nora, and they reluctantly hang up when he needs to go to class.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ NIGHT EST (DAY KST) ‑ TWO
scenes.summary: Nora showers, creates a Korean keyboard reference, and writes a long email to Hae Sung starting with how she never expected to meet him again.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung Skypes Nora from a coffee shop while she's just waking up at 7 AM, having adjusted her schedule to talk with him.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/SEOUL BUS ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora, dressed up for dinner, Skypes with Hae Sung who's on a bus to school, asking about each other's daily activities.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung teases Nora about her rusty Korean during their Skype call as she wakes up and he prepares for bed.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung asks if Nora still wants to win the Nobel Prize, and she says she's now interested in the Pulitzer.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung tells Nora she's the same as the 12-year-old in his memory - greedy and wanting everything, which makes them both laugh.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Nora explains her artist residency in Montauk to Hae Sung, referencing Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT KST
scenes.summary: Hae Sung watches Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind alone in his room.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL ‑ CLASSROOM ‑ MORNING EST
scenes.summary: Nora writes a long email to Hae Sung instead of doing her school assignment while Janice and Rachel peek at her screen.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Nora suggests Hae Sung come to New York to learn English instead of going to China to learn Mandarin.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ EVENING EST (MORNING KST)
scenes.summary: Nora asks if Hae Sung can hear her during a Skype call.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP ‑ MORNING KST (EVENING EST)
scenes.summary: Hae Sung's video is breaking up as he waits patiently for his screen to load in the coffee shop.

scenes.heading: INT. HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SEOUL ‑ EVENING KST (MORNING EST)
scenes.summary: Hae Sung asks if Nora can hear him during their Skype call from his room.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAD SCHOOL LIBRARY ‑ MORNING EST (EVENING KST)
scenes.summary: Nora impatiently waits for Hae Sung to load on her screen in the grad school library.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL COFFEE SHOP ‑ MORNING KST (EVENING EST)
scenes.summary: Hae Sung patiently waits for Nora to load on his screen in the coffee shop, but the call fails.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ MORNING EST (EVENING KST)
scenes.summary: Nora sleeps through Hae Sung's Skype calls and misses them as voice-overs reveal they've both asked when the other will visit.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. SEOUL CABLE CAR/NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK ‑
scenes.summary: Hae Sung shows Nora the Seoul skyline via Skype from a cable car, and she says she wishes she were there with him.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAE SUNG'S UNIVERSITY ‑ SEOUL ‑ AFTERNOON KST (LATE
scenes.summary: Hae Sung receives a message from Nora asking "Can we talk?" while smoking with friends outside his university.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S DORM ROOM IN NEW YORK/HAE SUNG'S ROOM IN SEOUL ‑
scenes.summary: Nora tells Hae Sung she's decided to focus on her writing and they agree to stop talking, both saying it would take over a year to visit each other anyway.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora and Hae Sung decide to end their reconnection to focus on their separate lives

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON JITNEY ‑ MONTAUK BOUND ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora naps curled up in her seat on a bus to Montauk, missing the views of New York City passing outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora arrives by cab at the artist residency barn house in Montauk where she will stay for a month.

scenes.heading: INT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ DOWNSTAIRS ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora enters the charming but empty residency house filled with art and books, then walks upstairs to find a room.

scenes.heading: INT. MONTAUK RESIDENCY BARN HOUSE ‑ UPSTAIRS ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nora finds two unoccupied bedrooms across from each other upstairs and chooses the obviously nicer one.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S ROOM ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nora enters her artist residency room at the barn house, writes her name on the wall with other past residents, and begins unpacking.

scenes.heading: INT. SEOUL SUBWAY TRAIN ‑ LATE NIGHT KST (AFTERNOON EST)
scenes.summary: Hae Sung writes a long email to Nora on a Seoul subway train but deletes it before getting off at his stop.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung arrives late to meet his friends at a soju bar where they ask about his upcoming trip, and he downs all the soju glasses on the table.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S ROOM ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Arthur arrives by cab at the barn house while Nora naps, but his face remains obscured as he walks toward the building.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN HOUSE ‑ NORA'S BEDROOM TO KITCHEN ‑ MIDDAY
scenes.summary: Nora wakes from her nap, notices Arthur's belongings in the room across the hall, and goes downstairs for coffee.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur meet for the first time outside the barn house, introducing themselves and making small talk about his room.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ NIGHT ‑ SOMETIME THAT SUMMER
scenes.summary: Nora explains the Korean concept of In-Yun to the other tipsy artists at the barn house during a summer evening.

scenes.heading: INT. PLANE TO SHANGHAI ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Hae Sung sits on a flight to Shanghai as Nora's voiceover continues explaining In-Yun.

scenes.heading: INT. SHANGHAI DORM ROOM ‑ DUSK
scenes.summary: Hae Sung sleeps in his small Shanghai dorm room with language exchange program materials on his bed.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHANGHAI NIGHT MARKET ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung walks through a Shanghai night market with other language exchange students while a cute Korean girl glances at him.

scenes.heading: INT. SHANGHAI NOODLE SHOP ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung and the cute Korean girl make eye contact and smile at each other over noodle soup while drinking with other students.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARN HOUSE PICNIC TABLE ‑ NIGHT ‑ SOMETIME THAT SUMMER
scenes.summary: Nora finishes explaining In-Yun to Arthur, suggesting they might have been connected in past lives as the other artists have left them alone.
scenes.turningPointNote: First romantic moment between Nora and Arthur, establishing their connection

scenes.heading: INT. TORONTO PEARSON AIRPORT ‑ A FEW DAYS LATER ‑ MORNING
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur answer questions from a CBP officer at Toronto airport, with Arthur speaking for both of them as a couple.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur make faces at each other across a New York street, then walk away hand-in-hand as Nora reveals they're married.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals Nora and Arthur are now married, jumping forward in time

scenes.heading: INT. REHEARSAL ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Nora watches a Korean-American actress perform a monologue from her play during rehearsal with her director and stage manager.

scenes.heading: INT. BOOKSTORE ‑ BROOKLYN ‑ LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: Arthur signs copies of his novel 'Boner' at a bookstore while Nora brings him lunch and gives him a quick kiss.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APT ‑ LIVING ROOM ‑ NEW YORK ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur finish having sex and discuss what to eat, with Arthur using his limited Korean phrases.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ LATER ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Arthur walk hand-in-hand as she mentions Hae Sung's upcoming visit and Arthur asks why he's coming.

scenes.heading: INT. ALL‑NIGHT SOJU BAR ‑ SEOUL ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: An older Hae Sung drinks with his friends in Seoul as they question whether he's going to New York to see his first love.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITY ‑ WHEN HAE SUNG ARRIVES
scenes.summary: Heavy rain pours down as New York welcomes Hae Sung with its wettest and saddest weather.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL LOBBY ‑ NEW YORK ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung arrives at his midtown hotel completely soaked from the rain, struggling with his suitcase as New Yorkers bump into him.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hae Sung opens the curtains in his hotel room to see the relentless rain continuing outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ AWNING AT THE ENTRANCE ‑ LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung stands under the hotel awning trying to light a cigarette while watching other tourists brave the rain with umbrellas.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ LOBBY ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Hae Sung gives up on sightseeing and lounges miserably in the hotel lobby watching TV and drinking as rain continues.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITYSCAPES ‑ EVENING TO NIGHT
scenes.summary: The rain finally stops like a miracle.

scenes.heading: EXT. MADISON SQUARE PARK ‑ NEXT DAY ‑ SUNRISE
scenes.summary: Hae Sung waits nervously at Madison Square Park until Nora appears and they see each other for the first time in twenty years, both looking shocked.
scenes.turningPointNote: The long-awaited reunion between Nora and Hae Sung after twenty years apart

scenes.heading: INT. MADISON SQUARE PARK SUBWAY STATION ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung walk down into the subway station, wait for the train, and board together.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY ‑ R TRAIN ‑ LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung stand together holding the same subway pole without touching, awkwardly but sweetly smiling at each other.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Nora tells Hae Sung she visited Korea with her husband and emailed him but got no response, while he apologizes and mentions he's not with his girlfriend currently.

scenes.heading: EXT. BY THE WATER ‑ BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung walk by the water surrounded by couples on dates, discussing her past relationship with Arthur in this location while trying to ignore the lovers around them.

scenes.heading: EXT. IN FRONT OF JANE'S CAROUSEL ‑ SOMETIME LATER ‑ SUNSET
scenes.summary: Nora asks Hae Sung why he looked for her twelve years ago, and he explains he wanted to see her one more time because he was upset she just left.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora tells Arthur that Hae Sung came to see her as Arthur's video game ends in defeat.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hae Sung returns alone to his small hotel room, sits on the bed, and looks out at the cityscape in silence.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BATHROOM ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora tells Arthur about how Korean Hae Sung is while getting ready for bed, and Arthur asks if Hae Sung is attractive.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BEDROOM ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Arthur admits he's mad but says he has no right to be, joking about Nora running away with Hae Sung as they continue preparing for bed.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDTOWN HOTEL ‑ HAE SUNG'S ROOM ‑ SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Hae Sung showers, gets into bed, and turns off the lights in his hotel room.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ BEDROOM ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Arthur and Nora lie in bed discussing how Hae Sung's story is more romantic than theirs, with Arthur calling himself the boring husband standing in the way of destiny.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: The empty Brooklyn Bridge Park at night appears impossibly beautiful and romantic with Manhattan lit up and some couples still making out.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINE FOR THE FERRY / SECURITY ‑ DAY
scenes.summary: Hae Sung waits in line for the Statue of Liberty ferry, beams when Nora arrives, and gives her a breakfast bagel he brought for her.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung board the ferry and stand at the railing looking at the water in an intolerably romantic setting.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Nora takes tourist photos of Hae Sung with his phone, and they politely decline when a family offers to photograph them together.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ BACK OF THE STATUE OF LIBERTY ‑ MOMENTS
scenes.summary: Hae Sung and Nora observe the Statue of Liberty from behind, with Hae Sung noting that she's turned against them.

scenes.heading: EXT. ON THE FERRY ‑ HEADING BACK TO MANHATTAN ‑ AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Nora shows Hae Sung photos of her wedding where she wore a Korean hanbok, and he comments that she looked young.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ LATER ‑ EVENING
scenes.summary: Hae Sung asks if Arthur knows he's coming over and who he is, with Nora confirming Arthur wants to meet him.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: Arthur waits anxiously in their cleaned apartment until Nora and Hae Sung arrive, and he's startled by how well they seem to fit together.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ NIGHT
scenes.summary: The three walk through East Village streets discussing their day, with Hae Sung surprised Arthur has never visited the Statue of Liberty.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE RESTAURANT ‑ MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Arthur, Nora, and Hae Sung eat pasta at a bustling restaurant, getting along wonderfully with Nora translating between them.

scenes.heading: INT. EAST VILLAGE BAR / SPEAKEASY ‑ LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: The three sit in the speakeasy booth from the opening scene, drinking cocktails and laughing as Hae Sung shares stories about military service and work in broken English.
scenes.turningPointNote: The key scene that frames the entire film, showing the three characters together at the emotional center of the story

scenes.heading: INT. NORA AND ARTHUR'S APARTMENT ‑ CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hae Sung picks up his bag to leave as Nora offers to walk him to his uber, with Arthur staying behind alone in their apartment.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ VERY LATE NIGHT ‑ ALMOST DAWN
scenes.summary: Nora and Hae Sung walk in heavy silence to his uber pickup location, wait for two excruciating minutes, then hug goodbye as he gets in the car.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final goodbye between Nora and Hae Sung, the emotional climax of their reunion

scenes.heading: EXT. NA YOUNG'S NEIGHBORHOOD ‑ PYOUNGCHON ‑ LATE‑90S ‑
scenes.summary: 12-year-old Na Young and Hae Sung stand facing each other in front of her childhood apartment building in Korea, recreating their first goodbye but now in the soft glow of late night instead of afternoon.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST VILLAGE STREET ‑ 5 A.M. ‑ ALMOST DAWN
scenes.summary: Hae Sung asks Nora in Korean what if this is also a past life and who they might be to each other in their next life, says goodbye, gets in his uber and drives away, leaving Nora alone on the street where she begins crying harder with each step as she walks home.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final goodbye between Nora and Hae Sung - the central relationship conflict resolves as they part forever, with Nora's emotional breakdown revealing the true cost of her choice

scenes.heading: INT. UBER ‑ DAWN
scenes.summary: Hae Sung sits alone in the back seat of the uber as it drives him away from Nora through the waking city, feeling both massive and small as he faintly smiles and watches New York pass by his window.

structuralWarnings.text: All 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
